---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(tidyverse)
options(readr.show_col_types = F)
library(here)
library(caret)
library(glmnet)
library(randomForest)
library(Metrics)
library(mgcv)
library(future)
library(furrr)
library(progressr)
```

```{r}
dailyDatRoll <- read_csv(here("Data/dailyDatRoll.csv"))
```

```{r}
# Define predictor and response variables
predictor_vars <- c(
  "percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change",
  "AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", 
  "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d", "meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d",
  "meanQ_ecdf", "WaterYear"
)
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "probHypSat")

# Generate all combinations of predictor variables
all_combs <- unlist(
  lapply(2:4, function(k) combn(predictor_vars, k, simplify = FALSE)),
  recursive = FALSE
)

# Inspect the generated combinations
comb_df <- data.frame(
  Combination = seq_along(all_combs),
  Predictors = sapply(all_combs, function(x) paste(x, collapse = ", "))
)

# Prepare data
modelDat <- dailyDatRoll %>% 
  select(-minDO.obs, -maxDO.obs, -avgDO.obs, -DO.hyp, -DO.amp, -probHyp) %>% 
  drop_na() %>% 
  mutate(WaterYear = factor(WaterYear, levels = unique(WaterYear)))

# Reshape data to long format
data_long <- modelDat %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(response_vars), names_to = "Response", values_to = "Value")

# Plan for parallel processing
plan(multisession, workers = 4) # Use multiple R sessions; you can also use multicore for Unix-based systems

# Enable progress tracking
handlers("txtprogressbar") # Use a global progress handler (e.g., progress bar)

# Function to process combinations in parallel with progress tracking
parallel_fit_with_progress <- function(all_combs, data_long, response_interaction = "Response * WaterYear") {
  # Function to fit the GAM for a given predictor set
  fit_gam <- function(predictors) {
    # Build the formula
    formula <- as.formula(paste(
      "Value ~", 
      paste(paste0("s(", predictors, ")"), collapse = " + "), 
      "+", response_interaction
    ))
    
    # Try fitting the GAM, handle errors gracefully
    result <- tryCatch({
      gam_model <- gam(formula, data = data_long, method = "REML")
      list(model = gam_model, AIC = AIC(gam_model))
    }, error = function(e) {
      list(model = NULL, AIC = NA) # Return NA for AIC in case of error
    })
    
    return(result)
  }

  # Add a progress bar
  p <- progressor(along = all_combs) # Initialize progress tracking
  
  # Fit GAMs in parallel with progress reporting
  comb_results <- future_map(all_combs, ~ {
    p(sprintf("Processing combination %s", paste(.x, collapse = ", "))) # Update progress
    predictors <- .x
    result <- fit_gam(predictors)
    list(predictors = predictors, result = result)
  }, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)) # Ensures reproducibility
  
  return(comb_results)
}

# Track progress during execution
with_progress({
  comb_results <- parallel_fit_with_progress(all_combs, data_long)
})

# Summarize the results
results_summary <- data.frame(
  Predictors = sapply(comb_results, function(x) paste(x$predictors, collapse = ", ")),
  AIC = sapply(comb_results, function(x) x$result$AIC)
)

# Sort by AIC
results_summary <- results_summary %>% arrange(AIC)

# Display the top models
head(results_summary)

```

```{r}
# Define predictor and response variables
predictor_vars <- c(
  "percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change",
  "AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", 
  "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d", "meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d",
  "meanQ_ecdf", "WaterYear"
)
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "probHypSat")

# Define groups of variables
percent_wet_vars <- c("percent_wet", "perc_wet7d", "perc_wet14d", "perc_wet30d", "perc_wet60d", "perc_wet_change")
ppt_vars <- c("AvgPPT_7d", "AvgPPT_14d", "AvgPPT_30d", "totPPT_7d", "totPPT_14d", "totPPT_30d")
q_vars <- c("meanQ", "meanQ_7d", "meanQ_14d", "meanQ_30d", "meanQ_ecdf")

# Function to generate valid combinations
generate_valid_combinations <- function(percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars, other_vars, k_min = 2, k_max = 4) {
  all_combs <- list()
  
  for (k in k_min:k_max) {
    # Ensure k is large enough to include at least one variable from each group
    if (k < 3) next
    
    # For each combination size, pick one from each group
    percent_wet_combs <- combn(percent_wet_vars, 1, simplify = FALSE)
    ppt_combs <- combn(ppt_vars, 1, simplify = FALSE)
    q_combs <- combn(q_vars, 1, simplify = FALSE)
    
    # Remaining slots for "other" variables
    other_slots <- k - 3
    if (other_slots > 0 && length(other_vars) >= other_slots) {
      other_combs <- combn(other_vars, other_slots, simplify = FALSE)
    } else if (other_slots == 0) {
      other_combs <- list(NULL) # No additional variables needed
    } else {
      next # Skip if there aren't enough variables to fill other_slots
    }
    
    # Combine one from each group and all combinations of "other" variables
    for (pw in percent_wet_combs) {
      for (ppt in ppt_combs) {
        for (q in q_combs) {
          for (others in other_combs) {
            all_combs <- append(all_combs, list(c(unlist(pw), unlist(ppt), unlist(q), unlist(others))))
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(all_combs)
}

# Separate other variables
other_vars <- setdiff(predictor_vars, c(percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars))

# Generate constrained combinations
all_combs <- generate_valid_combinations(percent_wet_vars, ppt_vars, q_vars, other_vars)

# Format combinations for inspection
comb_df <- data.frame(
  Combination = seq_along(all_combs),
  Predictors = sapply(all_combs, function(x) paste(unlist(x), collapse = ", "))
)


# Prepare data
modelDat <- dailyDatRoll %>% 
  select(-minDO.obs, -maxDO.obs, -avgDO.obs, -DO.hyp, -DO.amp, -probHyp) %>% 
  drop_na() %>% 
  mutate(WaterYear = factor(WaterYear, levels = unique(WaterYear)))

# Reshape data to long format
data_long <- modelDat %>%
  select(all_of(c(predictor_vars, response_vars))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(response_vars), names_to = "Response", values_to = "Value")

# Plan for parallel processing
plan(multisession, workers = 4) # Use multiple R sessions; you can also use multicore for Unix-based systems

# Enable progress tracking
handlers("txtprogressbar") # Use a global progress handler (e.g., progress bar)

# Function to process combinations in parallel with progress tracking
parallel_fit_with_progress <- function(all_combs, data_long) {
  # Function to fit the GAM for a given predictor set
  fit_gam <- function(predictors) {
    # Build the formula
    formula <- as.formula(paste(
      "Value ~", 
      paste(paste0("s(", predictors, ")"), collapse = " + ")
    ))
    
    # Try fitting the GAM, handle errors gracefully
    result <- tryCatch({
      gam_model <- gam(formula, data = data_long, method = "REML")
      list(model = gam_model, AIC = AIC(gam_model))
    }, error = function(e) {
      list(model = NULL, AIC = NA) # Return NA for AIC in case of error
    })
    
    return(result)
  }

  # Add a progress bar
  p <- progressor(along = all_combs) # Initialize progress tracking
  
  # Fit GAMs in parallel with progress reporting
  comb_results <- future_map(all_combs, ~ {
    p(sprintf("Processing combination %s", paste(.x, collapse = ", "))) # Update progress
    predictors <- .x
    result <- fit_gam(predictors)
    list(predictors = predictors, result = result)
  }, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)) # Ensures reproducibility
  
  return(comb_results)
}

# Track progress during execution
with_progress({
  comb_results <- parallel_fit_with_progress(all_combs, data_long)
})

# Summarize the results
results_summary <- data.frame(
  Predictors = sapply(comb_results, function(x) paste(x$predictors, collapse = ", ")),
  AIC = sapply(comb_results, function(x) x$result$AIC)
)

# Sort by AIC
results_summary <- results_summary %>% arrange(AIC)

# Display the top models
head(results_summary)
```

```{r}
best_model <- comb_results[[1]]$result$model

predictions <- predict(best_model, newdata = data_long)
ggplot(data_long, aes(x = Value, y = predictions)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Observed vs. Predicted Values",
    x = "Observed Value",
    y = "Predicted Value"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
options(readr.show_col_types = F)
library(here)
library(rsample)
library(furrr)
options(parallelly.fork.enable = T)
library(progressr)
library(purrr)

dailyDat <- read_csv("~/Desktop/dailyDat.csv")


# Step 1: Define response and predictor variables
response_vars <- c("minDO.pctsat", "maxDO.pctsat", "avgDO.pctsat", "DOpctsat.amp", "probHypSat", "PC1", "PC2", "cluster")
predictor_vars <- c(
  "PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d",
  "PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d", 
  "Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d", "exeProb",
  "Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d", "WaterYear"
)

# Step 2: Correct and define the grouping structure
grouped_predictors <- list(
  perc_wet = c("PercWet_1d", "PercWet_7d", "PercWet_14d", "PercWet_30d", "PercWet_60d"),
  PPT = c("PPT_1d", "PPT_7d", "PPT_14d", "PPT_30d", "PPT_60d"),
  Q = c("Q_1d", "Q_7d", "Q_14d", "Q_30d", "Q_60d"),
  exeProb = c("exeProb"),
  #quantQ = c("quantQ", "quantileQ_7d", "quantileQ_14d", "quantileQ_30d"),
  Depth = c("Depth_1d", "Depth_7d", "Depth_14d", "Depth_30d", "Depth_60d")
)

# Step 3: Generate combinations efficiently
generate_filtered_combos <- function() {
  all_combos <- map(2:4, ~combn(predictor_vars, .x, simplify = FALSE)) %>%
    purrr::flatten()
  
  filtered_combos <- purrr::keep(all_combos, function(combo) {
    # Check if no more than 1 variable is selected from each group
    group_counts <- sapply(grouped_predictors, function(group) sum(combo %in% group))
    valid_groups <- all(group_counts <= 1)
    valid_groups   # Allow only valid combinations
  })

  return(filtered_combos)
}

# Step 4: Generate the valid predictor combinations
predictor_combos <- generate_filtered_combos()
# Step 5: Create functions to run models
run_model <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_type = "linear") {
  # Ensure WaterYear is a factor in both datasets
  train_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(train_data$WaterYear)
  test_data$WaterYear <- as.factor(test_data$WaterYear)
  
  # Apply transformations based on model type
  if (model_type == "log-log") {
    train_data <- train_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(c(response, predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(c(response, predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))

  } else if (model_type == "log-linear") {
    train_data <- train_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(c(predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(c(predictors)) & -WaterYear, ~ log(. + 1)))
  } else if (model_type == "linear-log") {
    train_data <- train_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(response), ~ log(. + 1)))
    test_data <- test_data %>%
      mutate(across(all_of(response), ~ log(. + 1)))
  }
  
  # Define the formula
  lmForm <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", "(", paste(predictors, collapse = " + "), ")^2"))
  
  # Fit the model using lm()
  model <- lm(lmForm, data = train_data)
  
  # Make predictions
  predictions <- predict(model, newdata = test_data)
  
  # Back-transform predictions if necessary
  if (model_type %in% c("log-log", "log-linear")) {
    predictions <- exp(predictions) - 1
  }
  
  # Calculate RMSE and R-squared
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((predictions - test_data[[response]])^2))
  r_squared <- summary(model)$r.squared

  list(
    model = model, rmse = rmse, r_squared = r_squared,
    predictors = predictors, response = response, model_type = model_type
  )
}

# Step 6: Prepare data
metDat_model <- dailyDat %>%
  ungroup() %>% 
  select(all_of(c(response_vars, predictor_vars))) %>% 
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(WaterYear = as.factor(WaterYear)) %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ scale(.)[, 1]))

set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
data_split <- initial_split(metDat_model, prop = 0.7)
train_data <- training(data_split)
test_data <- testing(data_split)

# Step 7: Run models for each response and predictor combination with different types (parallelized)
# Dynamically allocate cores, leaving 1 core free
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
plan(multicore, workers = num_cores) # Enable progress reporting
handlers("cli")         # Displays progress in the console

# Define a function for a single model run to be used with furrr
run_all_models <- function(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_types) {
  lapply(model_types, function(model_type) {
    run_model(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_type)
  })
}

# Define model_types globally before the parallel map
model_types <- c("linear", "log-log", "log-linear", "linear-log")

# Wrap the execution in a progress tracking context
with_progress({
  p <- progressor(steps = length(response_vars) * length(predictor_combos))
  
  # Parallelized execution with furrr
  results <- future_map(
    response_vars, 
    function(response) {
      future_map(
        predictor_combos, 
        function(predictors) {
          p()  # Increment progress
          run_all_models(response, predictors, train_data, test_data, model_types)
        }
      )
    }
  )
})


# Step 8: Extract results into dataframes (unchanged)
extract_results <- function(results_list) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(results_list, function(res) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(res, function(model_res) {
      do.call(rbind, lapply(model_res, function(model_result) {
        data.frame(
          response = model_result$response,
          predictors = paste(model_result$predictors, collapse = ", "),
          model_type = model_result$model_type,
          rmse = model_result$rmse,
          r_squared = model_result$r_squared
        )
      }))
    }))
  }))
}

results <- extract_results(results)

# Step 10: Rank models and analyze
bestLM <- results %>%
  group_by(response, model_type) %>%
  arrange(response, model_type, rmse, r_squared) %>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

# Shut down the parallel backend when done
plan(sequential)
```
